div.h1: A file's journey — from the guest to durable storage

div.p: A guest program just reads and writes files like normal. Underneath, this binary turns each operation into one broker call. The bytes only ever travel through one unix socket, and the guest never holds a key.

ul.li: A warm read never leaves the guest — that is why the end-to-end gate proves the broker path by reading the broker's own store, not the local cache.
If the broker is throttling, the upload backs off and retries — the bytes are never lost, just delayed (SEC-46).

text.tspan: asks for the bytes by uuid
in the local cache - close returns
